name: apple
outline: Round fruit with a broad lower body, slight top notch, and a short stem or small leaf bump at the top.
grayscale_cues: Mostly a solid bulb shape with one small protrusion above the center; not sharply faceted and not strongly elongated.
disambiguation: More rounded and fuller than ruby. Not crescent-shaped like banana and not narrow like mermaid hair or unicorn horn.

name: banana
outline: Short thick crescent fruit with a smooth outer arc, open inner curve, and both tips curving upward.
grayscale_cues: A bent crescent with a fat middle and no straight shaft, no rigid cone body, and no spear-like point progression from base to tip.
disambiguation: For colored images, strongly prefer banana when the icon is bright yellow. Do not choose unicorn horn unless the object is mostly straight, rigid, and tapered from a thick base to a sharp tip.

name: chip
outline: Small tilted rectangular or square token with straight edges, clipped corners, and a flat tech-like body.
grayscale_cues: Looks mechanical rather than round: straight sides, hard corners, inner square-like regions, and no circular medallion silhouette.
disambiguation: Choose chip only when the icon reads as a small angular circuit-like token. Do not choose chip for a round or nearly round purple-and-gold medallion; those should prefer epic coin. Do not choose chip when the icon looks like a green eye or green frame with a dark pupil-like center; those should prefer zombie eye.

name: coin
outline: Simple round coin disc with a smooth outer rim.
grayscale_cues: Near-perfect circle with a plain center and a single horizontal band or slot-like marking across the middle.
disambiguation: Simpler and smoother than epic coin, which has extra rings, heavier ornament, and a more complex interior.

name: dragon scale
outline: Curved shard or scale with one pointed tip and a wider rounded base.
grayscale_cues: Asymmetric leaf-like piece with segmented ridges inside; neither circular nor bottle-shaped.
disambiguation: Unlike chip it stays organic and curved instead of square and mechanical. Unlike wolf skin it is one compact scale, not a ragged hide.

name: epic coin
outline: Round or nearly round medallion-like coin with a thick outer border, ornate interior, and a fuller emblem silhouette than a plain coin.
grayscale_cues: Reads as a circular medallion with layered rim detail, bright star-like highlights, and a busy center; not as a rectangle or clipped square.
disambiguation: Choose epic coin for the purple-and-gold circular emblem with sparkle or star highlights. Prefer it over chip whenever the icon is fundamentally round rather than angular.

name: epic fish
outline: Long slim fish body with a pointed head and more aggressive fins.
grayscale_cues: Most elongated of the fish icons, with a narrow body, sharper nose, and a more pronounced spiky tail or fins.
disambiguation: Longer and more blade-like than normie fish. Less round and less fan-tailed than golden fish.

name: golden fish
outline: Compact fish with a prominent fan-like tail and a visible eye on the body.
grayscale_cues: Fish silhouette with a fuller tail spread and clearer eye/body separation than normie fish.
disambiguation: Broader tail and more decorative profile than normie fish. Shorter and less sleek than epic fish.

name: life potion
outline: Bottle with a narrow neck and stopper above a wide rounded body and flat base.
grayscale_cues: Very recognizable bottle silhouette: thin vertical neck on top and large oval or flask body underneath.
disambiguation: Unlike coin or apple it has a clear neck. Unlike zombie eye it is vertical and bottle-shaped, not horizontal and oval.

name: mermaid hair
outline: Single flowing lock of hair that curls downward like a hooked wave or long comma.
grayscale_cues: One thick curved strand with a sweeping curl; no forked end and no straight spike body.
disambiguation: More like a curling lock than banana. Softer and more looped than unicorn horn, which is straighter and conical. For colored images it should not beat banana when the icon is a clearly yellow fruit crescent.

name: normie fish
outline: Short simple fish with a compact oval body and small tail.
grayscale_cues: Squat fish silhouette with a blunt body center and a modest fork or tail, without ornate fins.
disambiguation: Simpler and rounder than both golden fish and epic fish. Choose this when the fish looks basic and compact.

name: ruby
outline: Faceted gemstone with a symmetric diamond-like body and pointed top and bottom.
grayscale_cues: Hard angular silhouette with flat facets and strong symmetry, not rounded like fruit or coins.
disambiguation: Sharper and more geometric than apple. Not circular like coin and not bottle-shaped like life potion.

name: unicorn horn
outline: Rigid diagonal spike or horn with one thick base, one narrow sharp tip, and a mostly straight or only slightly bent body.
grayscale_cues: Reads like a conical spear or horn, not like a rounded crescent fruit. The width should taper progressively from base to tip.
disambiguation: Never choose unicorn horn for a strongly yellow crescent-shaped fruit. Choose it only when the icon is spear-like, rigid, and clearly not banana-shaped.

name: wolf skin
outline: Irregular pelt or hide with ragged edges, a broad top, and torn lower flaps.
grayscale_cues: Messy hide silhouette with multiple corners and cutouts, wider than tall and not a single clean geometric object.
disambiguation: Unlike dragon scale it is a ragged skin, not one smooth shard. Unlike chip it is organic and uneven rather than angular and mechanical.

name: zombie eye
outline: Eye-like icon with a dark central pupil or iris area inside a bright green outer shape; it can read as a tilted eye, diamond, or square frame around the pupil.
grayscale_cues: High-contrast center-with-ring structure: one dark square or round pupil inside a lighter inner block and a brighter outer green frame, even when the overall silhouette is slightly tilted.
disambiguation: Choose zombie eye when the icon reads like an eye or pupil inside a green frame, even if the outer shape looks like a tilted square. Prefer it over chip whenever there is a clear dark pupil-like center with a surrounding light-and-green border.
